terminal.c(3) Library Functions Manual terminal.c(3)

NAME

terminal.c - Terminal function stubs for WASM (not needed for mirror mode).

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t terminal_get_size (terminal_size_t *size)
asciichat_error_t terminal_cursor_hide (void)
asciichat_error_t terminal_cursor_show (void)
unsigned short int terminal_get_effective_width (void)
unsigned short int terminal_get_effective_height (void)

Detailed Description

Terminal function stubs for WASM (not needed for mirror mode).

Definition in file platform/wasm/stubs/terminal.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t terminal_cursor_hide (void )

17   return ASCIICHAT_OK;

unsigned
  short int terminal_get_effective_height (void )


Definition at line 35 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/terminal.c.


35                                                        {
36   int height = GET_OPTION(height);
37   if (height > 0) {
38     return (unsigned short int)height;
39   }
40   // For WASM, use default since terminal detection is stubbed
41   return OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT;
42 }










unsigned
  short int terminal_get_effective_width (void )


Definition at line 25 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/terminal.c.


25                                                       {
26   int width = GET_OPTION(width);
27   if (width > 0) {
28     return (unsigned short int)width;
29   }
30   // For WASM, use default since terminal detection is stubbed
31   return OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT;
32 }










asciichat_error_t
  terminal_get_size (terminal_size_t * size)


Definition at line 11 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/terminal.c.


11                                                            {
12   (void)size;
13   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED, "Terminal operations not supported in WASM");
14 }






References SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by action_show_capabilities_immediate(),
    options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(),
    options_print_help_for_mode(),
    sdp_detect_terminal_capabilities(),
    terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(), and
  terminal_screen_render().
